revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikuta Shrine]][[th:ศาลเจ้าอิกูตะ]] [[de:Ikuta-Schrein]] [[en:Ikuta Shrine]] [[es:Santuario Ikuta]] [[eu:Ikuta santutegia]] [[fr:Ikuta-jinja]] [[it:Santuario di Ikuta]] [[ja:生田神社]] [[simple:Ikuta Shrine]] [[zh:生田神社]] {{Expand Japanese|生田神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikuta Shrine (Q710086) | native_name = 生田神社 | image = Ikuta-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikuta Shrine (Q710086) | coordinates = {{coord|34.694766|135.190691}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Wakahiru-me|lt=Wakahirume|en|Wakahiru-me|ja|稚日女尊|zh|稚日女尊|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}} | website = {{Official website|http://www.ikutajinja.or.jp}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikuta Shrine'''|生田神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Q845945|ltq=Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q10898274|ltq=Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q135009157|ltq=Q135009157|qid=Q135009157|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai and Ainame-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Kanpei-sha|lt=Kanpei-sha|simple|Kanpei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q10444949|ltq=Q10444949|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}} ** Series ordinal: 66 * {{ill|Q11498550|ltq=Q11498550|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 54 ** P155: {{ill|Hokura Shrine|lt=Hokura Shrine|ja|保久良神社|qid=Q17218056|1=Hokura Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q661395|ltq=Q661395|qid=Q661395|1=Nagata Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q686802|ltq=Q686802|qid=Q686802|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Yatabe district|lt=Yatabe district|ja|八部郡|zh|八部郡|qid=Q11391769|1=Yatabe district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.694766|135.190691}} == official website (P856) == * http://www.ikutajinja.or.jp ** Language of work: {{ill|Japanese language|lt=Japanese|ab|Аиапон бызшәа|ace|Bahsa Jeupang|ady|Японыбзэ|af|Japannees|als|Japanische Sprache|ami|Sowal no dipong|am|ጃፓንኛ|ang|Iapanisc sprǣc|anp|जापानी भाषा|an|Idioma chaponés|arc|ܠܫܢܐ ܝܦܢܝܐ|ar|اللغة ნინა|yi|יאפאניש|yo|Èdè Japaní|za|Vah Nyiedbonj|zgh|ⵜⵓⵜⵍⴰⵢⵜ ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} * https://ikutajinja.or.jp/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Ikuta-jinja, haiden-1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Wakahiru-me|lt=Wakahirume|en|Wakahiru-me|ja|稚日女尊|zh|稚日女尊|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}}https://ikutajinja.or.jp/history == founded by (P112) == * {{ill|Q11558664|ltq=Q11558664|qid=Q11558664|1=Unagami no Isachi}} == CiNii Research ID (P11496) == * 1140563741654096384 == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: https://web.archive.org/web/20041124114702/http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp:80/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=50601 == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181074 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917277|ltq=Q134917277|qid=Q134917277|1=Kanpei Chūsha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Senior Fifth Rank|lt=Senior Fifth Rank|ja|正五位|ko|정5위|zh|正五位|qid=Q11123280|1=Senior Fifth Rank}} * {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank|ja|従四位|ko|종4위|zh|從四位|qid=Q11071127|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} * {{ill|Junior Third Rank|lt=Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|ko|종3위|zh|從三位|qid=Q11071123|1=Junior Third Rank}} == present in work (P1441) == * {{ill|Ikuta Atsumori|lt=Ikuta Atsumori|en|Ikuta Atsumori|fr|Ikuta Atsumori|qid=Q17049663|1=Ikuta Atsumori}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q3193781|ltq=Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いくたじんじゃ == Instagram username (P2003) == * ikuta_jinja == Facebook username (P2013) == * ikutajinja == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 131364153 == Library of Congress authority ID (P244) == * n81142853 == NACSIS-CAT author ID (P271) == * DA03061340CiNii Research == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 550730 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7140005000198 == nighttime view (P3451) == * Ikuta Jinja(main shrine).jpg == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00284443 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikuta Shrine == panoramic view (P4291) == * Ikuta-Jinja Honden4.jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '兵庫県神戸市中央区下山手通1丁目2-1', 'language': 'ja'} == Freebase ID (P646) == * /m/05gdndhttps://developers.google.com/freebase/ == commissioned by (P88) == * {{ill|Q232803|ltq=Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} == museum-digital place ID (P9957) == * 23707 {{commons category|Category:Ikuta Shrine}} [[DE:Ikuta-Schrein]] [[EN:Ikuta Shrine]] [[ES:Santuario Ikuta]] [[EU:Ikuta santutegia]] [[FR:Ikuta-jinja]] [[IT:Santuario di Ikuta]] [[JA:生田神社]] [[SIMPLE:Ikuta Shrine]] [[TH:ศาลเจ้าอิกูตะ]] [[ZH:生田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q710086}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Ikuta Shrine |Image = [[File:Ikuta-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kobe City|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Kobe City)|Q686802|qid=Q686802|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|lt=Shimoyamate-dōri|Q11360820|qid=Q11360820|1=Shimo-Yamate-dōri}} 1-chōme 2-1 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Wakahirume no Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanpei-chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyo Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |Founded = Year 1 of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} ({{ill|lt=201|Q30387|qid=Q30387|1=201}}) |Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |Pilgrimage = {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|Q10444949|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}} No. 66 (Hyōgo No. 1) |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=April 15|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} |Map = {{Location map|Kobe|label=Ikuta Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Ikuta-jinja, otorii-2.jpg|250px|thumb|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Ikuta Shrine''' (いくたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Chūō Ward (Kobe City)|Q686802|qid=Q686802|lt=Chūō Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Kobe City|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanpei-chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Along with {{ill|lt=Hirota Shrine|Q1466105|qid=Q1466105|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}} and {{ill|lt=Nagata Shrine|Q661395|qid=Q661395|1=Nagata Shrine}}, it is a shrine with a history dating back to {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, and together with Nagata Shrine and {{ill|lt=Minatogawa Shrine|Q713188|qid=Q713188|1=Minatogawa Shrine}} it is one of the representative shrines of Kobe. In the past, the area that is now central Kobe City was shrine land, which became the origin of the place name “Kobe.” Locally, it is affectionately known as “Ikuta-san.” === Enshrined Deity === * Main enshrined deity – {{ill|lt=Wakahirume no Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}} (わかひるめのみこと){{Cite web |url=https://www.city.kobe.lg.jp/documents/44019/d-10_ikutajinja.pdf |title=History of Chūō Ward: Historic Sites and Place Names – Ikuta Shrine|access-date=2024-11-30|publisher=Kobe City}}. The name means “the young and fresh sun goddess,” and she is said to be either the childhood name or the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=nigi-mitama|1=UNKNOWN}} (gentle spirit) of {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, or her younger sister deity. === History === According to the {{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}, in the first year of {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} ({{ill|lt=201|Q30387|qid=Q30387|1=201}}), while the Empress was returning after completing the {{ill|lt=Silla conquest|Q11249435|qid=Q11249435|1=Three Han Campaign}}, her ship stopped moving at what is now {{ill|lt=Kobe Port|Q1336906|qid=Q1336906|1=Port of Kobe}}. She performed divination, whereupon {{ill|lt=Wakahirume no Mikoto|Q11596349|qid=Q11596349|1=Wakahiru-me}} appeared and delivered an oracle saying, “I wish to dwell in the land of Ikuta Nagasa. Command {{ill|lt=Unagami no Isachi|Q11558664|qid=Q11558664|1=Unagami no Isachi}} to enshrine me in the land of Ikuta.” The Empress then appointed Unagami no Isachi as priest and had Wakahirume no Mikoto enshrined at {{ill|lt=Mount Nunobiki|Q11480792|qid=Q11480792|1=Mount Nunobiki}} (Sand Mountain), located behind the present {{ill|lt=Shin-Kobe Station|Q801435|qid=Q801435|1=Shin-Kōbe Station}}. This is said to be the beginning of the shrine[https://ikutajinja.or.jp/history Ikuta Shrine official website – History]. Although the shrine was originally on Sand Mountain, on April 9 of {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 18 ({{ill|lt=799|Q33488|qid=Q33488|1=799}}), a massive flood of {{ill|lt=Nunobiki Falls (Hyōgo)|Q3695449|qid=Q3695449|1=Nunobiki Falls}} caused the western edge of the mountain to collapse, damaging and tilting the shrine buildings. Tōne Shichidayū of Ikuta Village evacuated the sacred object and, carrying it on his back, searched for a new site for seven or eight days. When he reached Ikuta Forest, the sacred object suddenly became too heavy to carry further. Believing this to be the deity’s will, he enshrined it there—at the present location. In the first year of {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|qid=Q30021|1=806}}), the imperial court granted the shrine 44 households known as “Ikuta no Shinpu Yonjuuyonko,” households that provided offerings, care, and protection—{{ill|lt=Kambei|UNKNOWN|lt=Kobe}}. Since then, the area of present-day central Kobe City became shrine land. It is said that this “Kanbe” later changed to “Konbe,” and eventually came to be called “Kōbe,” forming the place name Kobe. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Ikuta Shrine, Yabe District, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}},” ranked as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, and noted as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami, Aname, and Niiname festivals. During the late {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, in the {{ill|lt=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Q2116658|qid=Q2116658|1=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani}} fought in February of {{ill|lt=Juei|Q598057|qid=Q598057|1=Juei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}}), {{ill|lt=Taira no Tomomori|Q736706|qid=Q736706|1=Taira no Tomomori}} established his main camp in Ikuta Forest[https://ikutajinja.or.jp/precinct Ikuta Shrine official website – Walking the Grounds]. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, the shrine was designated a prefectural shrine in {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4). Later, during the western imperial tour of {{ill|lt=Emperor Meiji|Q174231|qid=Q174231|1=Emperor Meiji}} in {{ill|lt=1885|Q7821|qid=Q7821|1=1885}} (Meiji 18), it was promoted to a Kanpei shrine, becoming a Kanpei-shōsha, and in {{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|qid=Q7846|1=1896}} (Meiji 29) it was further elevated to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kanpei-chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine buildings were damaged in the {{ill|lt=1938|Q18645|qid=Q18645|1=1938}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 13) {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin Flood|Q11656935|qid=Q11656935|lt=Kobe Flood|1=1938 Hanshin flood}}, destroyed by fire in the {{ill|lt=Kobe air raids|Q4940711|qid=Q4940711|1=Bombing of Kobe in World War II}} on March 17, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|qid=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), and damaged again in the {{ill|lt=Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|Q11392882|qid=Q11392882|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake}} of {{ill|lt=1995|Q2068|qid=Q2068|1=1995}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 7). Each time, it was rebuilt, leading to reverence for the shrine as that of a “reviving deity.” In January {{ill|lt=2000|Q1985|qid=Q1985|1=2000}} (Heisei 12), a “Ikuta Shrine Earthquake Reconstruction Monument” was erected on the west side of the main hall, with a waterproof pump installed behind it to draw water from the pond. Within the grounds are a monument to the Noh chant “Ikuta Atsumori,” a knife mound donated by local restaurants and food-related companies, and a sacred camphor tree ({{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|qid=Q158722|lt=kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}}). Many donor names written on the pillars belong to hotel or maritime-related companies. === Court Ranks === * {{ill|lt=Ten’an (Japan)|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten’an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, Eighth Order to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, Eighth Order ({{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 10 ({{ill|lt=868|Q25061|qid=Q25061|1=868}}) {{ill|lt=December 16|Q2410|qid=Q2410|1=December 16}}: Promoted from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, Eighth Order to Junior Third Rank, Eighth Order (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku) === Legends and Traditions === During the great flood of Enryaku 18 (799), pine trees ({{ill|lt=pine|Q12024|qid=Q12024|lt=matsu|1=Pine}}) had been planted around the shrine, but they were said to have been completely ineffective in preventing the flood. As a result, pines came to be disliked, and at New Year, cedar decorations are erected instead of {{ill|lt=kadomatsu|Q2667233|qid=Q2667233|1=Kadomatsu}}. It is also said that not a single pine tree is planted in Ikuta Forest. Near {{ill|lt=Kumochi Town|Q11567845|qid=Q11567845|1=Kumochi-chō}} in Chūō Ward, close to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kumochi Hachiman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, there is a torii known as the “Asahi Torii”{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301039.html|title=Kumochi Hachiman Shrine|publisher={{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|Q135249839|qid=Q135249839|1=Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Association}}|accessdate=2020-07-10}}, which is said to have been the first torii when Ikuta Shrine was located on Sand Mountain. It is called the “Asahi Torii” because it is said not to cast a shadow even when struck by the morning sun on New Year’s Day. The chief priest Takahisa Katō’s book *Ikuta Shrine* ({{ill|lt=Gakuseisha|Q11448819|qid=Q11448819|1=Gakuseisha}}, 2005) provides a detailed explanation including its history. In the Noh play *Ikuta Atsumori*, the surviving child of {{ill|lt=Taira no Atsumori|Q2284163|qid=Q2284163|lt=Atsumori|1=Taira no Atsumori}} encounters his deceased father here through prayers made to {{ill|lt=Kamomioya Shrine|Q701620|qid=Q701620|lt=Kamo Myōjin|1=Shimogamo Shrine}}. === Shrine Grounds === * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Knife Mound – Established on October 25, {{ill|lt=1993|Q2065|qid=Q2065|1=1993}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 5) * East Gate * Ikuta Forest – A {{ill|lt=Chinju no Mori|Q11651158|qid=Q11651158|1=Chinju no Mori}} located on the north side of the grounds. {{ill|lt=Taira no Tomomori|Q736706|qid=Q736706|1=Taira no Tomomori}} established his main camp here during the Battle of Ichi-no-Tani in February {{ill|lt=1184|Q19728|qid=Q19728|1=1184}}. * Northwest Gate * Shinkōtei Pavilion * Ikuta Pond – Benzaiten Pond, long known as a scenic spot * Shrine Office * Ikuta Shrine Hall * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Rōmon Gate * West Gate File:Ikuta-jinja, honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Ikuta-jinja, roumon-2.jpg|Rōmon Gate ファイル:Ikuta-jinja Kobe11bs4272.jpg|Ikuta Forest ファイル:Ikuta Jinja Ikuta Ike.jpg|Ikuta Pond File:Ikuta-jinja, torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Ikuta-jinja, ichi-no-torii.jpg|First Torii === Auxiliary Shrines === The following {{ill|lt=auxiliary and subordinate shrines|Q1071160|qid=Q1071160|1=Setsumatsusha}} are located within the grounds{{Cite web|ja|url=https://ikutajinja.or.jp/precinct|title=Walking the Grounds – Shrine Edition|publisher=Ikuta Shrine|accessdate=2020-07-10}}. The enshrined deities and descriptions are according to the official website. * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|Q11381857|qid=Q11381857|1=Sumiyoshi Shrine}} – Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Uwatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sokotsutsu-no-O-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Deities of maritime and traffic safety. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|qid=Q11390939}} – Deity: {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}. Deity of warding off misfortune and bringing good fortune. * {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine|Q2370082|qid=Q2370082|1=Suwa Shrine}} – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}}. Deity of agriculture, hunting, martial fortune, and strength. * {{ill|lt=Hiyoshi Shrine|Q11505065|qid=Q11505065|1=Hiyoshi Shrine}} – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. Deity of agriculture, industry, and abundant harvests. * {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Shrine|Q11480540|qid=Q11480540|1=Ichikishima Shrines}} – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}. Deity of performing arts and water. * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} – Deity: {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. Deity of livelihood protection, food, and rice. * Sai Shrine – Deities: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chigaeshi no Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachimatahiko-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yachimatahime-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}. Deities of disease prevention and road protection. * Ikazuchi Daijin Shrine – Deity: {{ill|lt=Nakatomi Ikatsu|Q55536814|qid=Q55536814|lt=Nakatomi Ikatsu no Muraji|1=Nakatomi no Ikatsu}}. Ancestral deity of the Gokō family, former shrine priests. * Hitomaro Shrine – Deity: {{ill|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|Q380818|qid=Q380818|lt=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro|1=Kakinomoto no Hitomaro}}. Deity of marital harmony and academic achievement. * Ebisu Shrine – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Deity of business prosperity. * Ōumi Shrine – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Deity of maritime safety, traffic safety, direction warding, and path opening. * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|qid=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}. Deity of sake brewing and agriculture/industry. A monument designates Ikuta Shrine as the “birthplace of {{ill|lt=Nada Gogo|Q6957369|qid=Q6957369|1=Nada-Gogō}} sake brewing,” and the {{ill|lt=tamagaki|Q3078312|qid=Q3078312|1=Sacred fences}} fence was maintained by sake brewers and merchants{{Cite web|ja|url=https://840.gnpp.jp/nadagogoshuzo/|title=Birthplace of Nada Gogo Sake Brewing|work=Birthplace Collection|date=2018-3|accessdate=2020-07-10}}. The monument records that, according to the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} “{{ill|lt=Genba-ryō|Q11572314|qid=Q11572314|1=Genba-ryō}},” when entertaining envoys from Silla at {{ill|lt=Minume Shrine|Q11499217|qid=Q11499217|lt=Minume Port|1=Minume Shrine}}, rice was gathered from Ikuta Shrine, {{ill|lt=Hirota Shrine|Q1466105|qid=Q1466105|1=Hirota Shrine (Osaka City)}}, {{ill|lt=Nagata Shrine|Q661395|qid=Q661395|1=Nagata Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Kataoka Shrine (Ōji Town)|Q43594918|qid=Q43594918|lt=Kataoka Shrine|1=Kataoka Shrine (Oji Town)}} in Yamato Province, and new sake brewed at Ikuta Shrine was to be used{{Cite web|ja|url=https://jp.sake-times.com/special/project/pr_tatenokawa_004|title=Was the origin of “Nada no Ki-Ippon” a divine oracle?! – The connection between Nada sake and three shrines|work=SAKETIMES|date=2019-02-21|accessdate=2020-07-10}}{{Cite web|ja|url=http://www.archives.city.amagasaki.hyogo.jp/chronicles/visual/01kodai/kodai1-3.html|title=The Yamato polity and the Ina River basin|work=Illustrated History of Amagasaki (Web Edition)|accessdate=2020-07-10}}. * Togakushi Shrine – Deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. Deity of physical health, strength enhancement, and great power. * Ikutamorizasusha – Deity: {{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}. Deity of safe childbirth, growth of all things, and guardian of Ikuta Forest. File:Ikuta-jinja, Ichikishima-jinja.jpg|Ichikishima Shrine File:Ikuta Inari shrine 2016b.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Ikuta-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Sai Shrine, Ikazuchi Daijin, Hitomaro Shrine File:Ikuta-jinja, Ebisu-jinja.jpg|Ebisu Shrine File:Ikuta-jinja, Daikai-jinja.jpg|Ōumi Shrine File:Ikuta-jinja, Matsuo-jinja.jpg|Matsuo Shrine File:Ikuta-jinja, Togakushi-jinja.jpg|Togakushi Shrine File:Ikuta-jinja Kobe13n4272.jpg|Ikutamorizasusha === Ikuta Forest === Behind the main hall lies Ikuta Forest (Ikuta no Mori), which is said to have once extended as far as the banks of the old Ikuta River. The forest appears in *{{ill|lt=The Pillow Book|Q615137|qid=Q615137|1=The Pillow Book}}*, and has been celebrated in many poems. * “Even the autumn wind cannot fully blow before the colors change—beneath the dew, the grasses of Ikuta Forest” ({{ill|lt=Fujiwara no Teika|Q378425|qid=Q378425|1=Fujiwara no Teika}}, *Shoku Gosen Wakashū*) * “In Ikuta Forest of Tsū Province, no one comes; through the night autumn wind, I speak to the moon” (Prince {{ill|lt=Munetaka|Q708897|qid=Q708897|1=Prince Munetaka}}, *Shin Gosen Wakashū*, Volume 5, Autumn) Because of its strategic position behind the Ikuta River, it was a battlefield during the {{ill|lt=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani|Q2116658|qid=Q2116658|1=Battle of Ichi-no-Tani}} of the Genpei War and the {{ill|lt=Battle of Minatogawa|Q114620|qid=Q114620|1=Battle of Minatogawa}} in the Nanboku-chō period. === Approach === The first torii stands at the southwestern end of Ikuta Road, southwest of {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Station|Q377926|qid=Q377926|1=Kobe-Sannomiya Station}}. From there, the approach passes through Center Street heading north, crosses Ikuta Shindō, and reaches the shrine name marker and second torii northeast of {{ill|lt=Hands (retail)|Q859212|qid=Q859212|lt=Hands|1=Hands (store)}}. Upon entering the grounds, there is a paid parking area, beyond which stand the third torii and the sacred gate. === Festivals === ;January – March * {{ill|lt=Hatsumōde|Q1032381|qid=Q1032381|1=Hatsumōde}} / {{ill|lt=New Year’s Day Festival|Q11546824|qid=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} ({{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}; mid-level festival) * Daily Offering Opening Festival – Okina Mask Hanging Ritual (January 2) – Also serves as an {{ill|lt=Noh mask|Q11612081|qid=Q11612081|lt=Okina mask|1=Noh mask}} ritual; early arrivals may view it up close * {{ill|lt=Genshisai|Q11387821|qid=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} (January 3; major festival) * Parishioners’ New Year Worship (around January 10) * {{ill|lt=Shimenawa|Q1887421|qid=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} Burning Ritual (January 16) * Setsubun Festival / {{ill|lt=Bean-throwing ritual|Q746798|qid=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} ({{ill|lt=February 3|Q2315|qid=Q2315|1=February 3}}) – Setsubun rites in the main hall; lucky beans are thrown from a special platform * Kigensai (February 11; mid-level festival) * {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (February 17; major festival) * Ikuta Inari Shrine Festival / Mochi-throwing Ritual (mid-{{ill|lt=March|Q110|qid=Q110|1=March}}) ;April – June * {{ill|lt=Kyokusui no Utage|Q5360526|qid=Q5360526|1=Winding stream party}} / Spring Tea Offering Festival (early {{ill|lt=April|Q118|qid=Q118|1=April}}) – Held in Ikuta Forest * Ikuta Festival Grand Rite / Parishioners’ Offering Festival (April 15; major festival) – Prayers for the safety and prosperity of parishioners and the Kobe region * Ikuta Festival Procession (Miwatari-shiki) (near the April grand festival) – A procession through the shrine’s surroundings and designated districts ;July – September * {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|qid=Q11261910|lt=Summer Great Purification|1=Ōharae-shiki}} (Chinowa ring passing) ({{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|qid=Q2686|1=July 15}}) – A purification ring is set up in the center of the approach * Road Offering Festival / Thousand Lantern Festival (July 15) – Held after the Summer Great Purification * Ōumi Shrine Summer Festival ({{ill|lt=August 3|Q2786|qid=Q2786|1=August 3}}: Eve Festival / 4th: Main Festival / 5th: Thanksgiving Festival) * Ancient Rite Festival (September 19; mid-level festival) * Autumn Grand Festival / Parishioners’ Offering Festival ({{ill|lt=September 20|Q2892|qid=Q2892|1=September 20}}; major festival) – Prayers for safety and prosperity * Autumn Festival Entertainment (Takigi Noh, Gagaku Evening, etc.) (September 19–23) * Autumn Tea Offering Festival (during the autumn festival period) * Flower Offering Festival (during the autumn festival period) ;October – December * Meiji Festival ({{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|qid=Q2995|1=November 3}}; mid-level festival) * {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 23; major festival) * Soot-Cleaning Festival ({{ill|lt=December 13|Q2379|qid=Q2379|1=December 13}}) * Emperor’s Birthday Festival (December 23) * Cedar Decoration Making (December 27) – A large cedar decoration made using about 2,000 cedar branches is offered * Year-End Great Purification (December 31) – A purification rite using human-shaped paper dolls * {{ill|lt=Road Offering Festival|Q21654797|qid=Q21654797|1=Michiae no matsuri}} / New Year’s Eve Festival (December 31) – Open to public participation === Branch Shrine === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ikuta Shrine Hyōgo Otabisho|1=UNKNOWN}} – {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Ward|Q1078790|qid=Q1078790|1=Hyōgo-ku, Kobe}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōhiraki-dōri|1=UNKNOWN}} 6-chōme{{Cite web|ja|url=https://ikutajinja.or.jp/otabi|title=Guardian Deity of Hyōgo – Ikuta Shrine Hyōgo Shrine Otabisho|publisher=Ikuta Shrine|accessdate=2020-07-10}}. === Events Held at the Shrine === * On {{ill|lt=February 4|Q2318|qid=Q2318|1=February 4}}, {{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|qid=Q2070|1=1996}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 8), the {{ill|lt=United States|Q30|qid=Q30|1=United States}} {{ill|lt=singer|Q177220|qid=Q177220|1=Singer}}, {{ill|lt=Cyndi Lauper|Q1545|qid=Q1545|1=Cyndi Lauper}}, participated in the {{ill|lt=Setsubun|Q746798|qid=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} {{ill|lt=bean-throwing|Q746798|qid=Q746798|1=Setsubun}} to慰問 and encourage victims of the Great Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake. * On {{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|qid=Q2341|1=February 17}}, {{ill|lt=2007|Q2024|qid=Q2024|1=2007}} (Heisei 19), the {{ill|lt=actress|Q33999|qid=Q33999|1=Actor}}, {{ill|lt=Norika Fujiwara|Q1352845|qid=Q1352845|1=Norika Fujiwara}}, and {{ill|lt=comedian|Q12330954|qid=Q12330954|1=Owarai tarento}}, {{ill|lt=Tomonori Jinnai|Q5358817|qid=Q5358817|1=Tomonori Jinnai}}, held their {{ill|lt=wedding|Q49836|qid=Q49836|1=Wedding}} here. To prevent confusion, the grounds and parking lot were closed and heavily guarded; the ceremony was closed to the public. They divorced two years later. * On {{ill|lt=December 7|Q2299|qid=Q2299|1=December 7}}, {{ill|lt=2018|Q25291|qid=Q25291|1=2018}} (Heisei 30), a dedication ceremony was held for a torii at Ikutamorizasusha within the grounds. The torii was made from {{ill|lt=asunaro|Q1330391|qid=Q1330391|1=Thujopsis dolabrata}} wood used for the “world’s largest {{ill|lt=Christmas tree|Q47128|qid=Q47128|1=Christmas tree}}” displayed the previous year at {{ill|lt=Meriken Park|Q5365128|qid=Q5365128|1=Meriken Park}} in {{ill|lt=Kobe City|Q48320|qid=Q48320|1=Kobe}}{{Cite web|ja|date=2018-12-07|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181224170457/https://this.kiji.is/443888666228147297 |title=Himi asunaro used for torii at Ikuta Shrine, Kobe|publisher=Hokuriku Shimbun |accessdate=2018-12-11}}. === Nearby Attractions, Historic Sites, Culture, and Tourist Facilities === {{col-begin}} {{col-2}} * {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Shrine (Kobe)|Q11355152|qid=Q11355152|lt=Sannomiya Shrine|1=Sannomiya Shrine (Kobe)}} * {{ill|lt=Nankinmachi (Kobe)|UNKNOWN|lt=Nankinmachi}} * {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefectural Guest House|Q11392878|qid=Q11392878|1=Hyogo Prefecture Guest House}} * {{ill|lt=Honganji Kobe Betsuin|Q11521062|qid=Q11521062|1=Hongan-ji Kobe Betsu-in Temple}} * {{ill|lt=Hashimizu Shrine (Kobe)|Q11636078|qid=Q11636078|lt=Hashimizu Shrine|1=Hashirimizu Shrine (Kobe)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kobe Mosque|1=UNKNOWN}} * Chinese Guild Hall ({{ill|lt=Kanteibyō|Q842400|qid=Q842400|1=Guandi Temple}}) * {{ill|lt=Kitano-chō Yamamoto-dōri|Q10855753|qid=Q10855753|1=Kitano-chō and Yamamoto-dōri}} * {{ill|lt=Hanakuma Castle|Q11615723|qid=Q11615723|1=Hanakuma Castle}} {{col-2}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shin-Kobe Ropeway|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kobe Nunobiki Herb Gardens|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Tokumitsu-in|Q11489541|qid=Q11489541|1=Tokkō-in Temple}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitano Promenade|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Sōraku-en|Q7667612|qid=Q7667612|1=Sōraku-en}} ** {{ill|lt=Former Hassam House|Q11510890|qid=Q11510890|1=Hassam House}} * {{ill|lt=Kobe Club|Q11589847|qid=Q11589847|1=Kobe Club}} * {{ill|lt=Tōa Road|Q11320988|qid=Q11320988|1=Tor Road}} * {{ill|lt=Ikuta Road|Q11259124|qid=Q11259124|1=Ikuta Road}} {{col-end}} === Adjacent Pilgrimage Sites === ; {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|Q10444949|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}} : 65 {{ill|lt=Katsuo-ji|Q2747522|qid=Q2747522|1=Katsuō-ji}} – '''66 Ikuta Shrine''' – 67 {{ill|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine|Q705297|qid=Q705297|1=Nishinomiya Shrine}} === Address === * 1-chōme 2-1 Shimoyamate-dōri, Chūō Ward, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture === Access === * About 1 minute on foot from {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Station (Kobe Municipal Subway)|Q111794954|qid=Q111794954|lt=Sannomiya Station|1=Kobe City Subway Sannomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kobe Municipal Subway|Q1049009|qid=Q1049009|1=Kobe Municipal Subway}} {{ill|lt=Seishin-Yamate Line|Q1066548|qid=Q1066548|1=Seishin-Yamate Line}}. * About 13 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Sannomiya-Hanadokeimae Station|Q6542103|qid=Q6542103|1=Sannomiya-Hanadokeimae Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kobe Municipal Subway Kaigan Line|Q844019|qid=Q844019|1=Kaigan Line}}. * About 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Station (Kobe New Transit)|Q111796671|qid=Q111796671|lt=Sannomiya Station|1=Sannomiya Station (Port Liner)}} on the {{ill|lt=Kobe New Transit|Q5366038|qid=Q5366038|1=Kobe New Transit}} {{ill|lt=Port Island Line|Q7230715|qid=Q7230715|1=Port Island Line}}. * About 9 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kobe-Sannomiya Station (Hanshin)|Q136216052|qid=Q136216052|lt=Kobe-Sannomiya Station|1=Kobe-Sannomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hanshin Electric Railway|Q1140781|qid=Q1140781|1=Hanshin Electric Railway}} {{ill|lt=Hanshin Main Line|Q1053647|qid=Q1053647|1=Hanshin Main Line}}. * About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Kobe-Sannomiya Station (Hankyu)|Q136216053|qid=Q136216053|lt=Kobe-Sannomiya Station|1=Kobe-Sannomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Hankyu Railway|Q473058|qid=Q473058|1=Hankyu}} {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kobe Main Line|Q1054520|qid=Q1054520|1=Hankyū Kōbe Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Hankyu Kobe Kosoku Line|Q11656795|qid=Q11656795|1=Hankyu Kobe Kosoku Line}}. * About 7 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Sannomiya Station|Q14616729|qid=Q14616729|1=Sannomiya Station (JR West)}} on the {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=JR Kobe Line|Q613302|qid=Q613302|1=JR Kōbe Line}}. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Related Books === * Motohiko Yasu & Yoshihiko Umeda {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōfūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1976, p. 558 * {{ill|lt=Eiji Shirai|Q11579387|qid=Q11579387|1=Eiji Shirai}} & Masanori Toki (eds.), *Shrine Dictionary*, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}}, 1992, pp. 198–199 * Takahisa Katō (ed. & author), *Great Encyclopedia of Shinto Funerary Rites*, {{ill|lt=Eikōshō Publishing|Q11495343|qid=Q11495343|1=Jukōshō Shuppan}}, 1997, p. 558 * Takahisa Katō (ed. & author), *Ikuta Shrine*, {{ill|lt=Gakuseisha|Q11448819|qid=Q11448819|1=Gakuseisha}}, 2005; revised edition 2019, p. 212 * Takahisa Katō (ed. & author), *Kumano Ōkami*, {{ill|lt=Eikōshō Publishing|Q11495343|qid=Q11495343|1=Jukōshō Shuppan}}, 2008, p. 558 * Takahisa Katō (ed. & author), *Ikuta Forest*, {{ill|lt=Kokusho Kankōkai|Q11420946|qid=Q11420946|1=Kokusho Kankōkai}}, 2010, p. 832 * Takahisa Katō (ed. & author), *Ikuta Forest and the Beginnings of Minato Kobe*, {{ill|lt=Eikōshō Publishing|Q11495343|qid=Q11495343|1=Jukōshō Shuppan}}, 2014, p. 127 === Related Items === {{Commonscat|Ikuta Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Ikuta Ejin Hachisha|Q11574819|qid=Q11574819|1=Ikuta Eishin Hachisha}} === External Links === * [http://www.ikutajinja.or.jp/ Ikuta Shrine] – Official website * {{Wayback|url=https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/13aede0510cc3110e781708ca25e679c |title=Ikuta Shrine Hyōgo Shrine (Otabisho) |date=20120129041749}} {{Shinto navigation}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikuta Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:生田神社|!]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kobe]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Kanpei Chūsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:三宮]] [[Category:Pilgrimage Route of Shinto and Buddhist Sacred Sites]] [[Category:Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Kobe]] [[Category:History of Chuo-ku, Kobe]]
